# Page 1^page=1
The Industrial Data Ecosystem Manufacturing-X Make Data Work. Competitive, Resilient & Sustainable -Talk Edition 25_6 │ 5. Juni 2025 Bitte beachten Sie: Dieser Talk wird aufgezeichnet. Bitte schalten Sie daher Kameras aus und Mikrofone stumm. Vielen Dank.

# Page 2^page=2
FORMAT ▪ Offenes Informations- und Austausch-Format ▪ Digitale Veranstaltung (Teams Meeting) ▪ Uhrzeit: 8:00 bis 9:00 Uhr ▪ Turnus: einmal monatlich, jeweils am ersten Donnerstag des Monats THEMA HEUTE Der Manufacturing-X Port – Das Architekturkonzept für branchen- übergreifende Interoperabilität 2 In dieser monatlichen Austauschrunde informieren wir Sie über die relevanten, aktuellen Entwicklungen zu Manufacturing-X und bieten der Community die Möglichkeit zum gemeinsamen, bereichernden Dialog. 00 | Herzlich Willkommen zum MX-Talk

# Page 11^page=11
FX-Port generic concept for vertical and horizontal integration 5 June 2025 8 FX- Port 4 FX- Port 3 FX- Port 2 FX- Port 1 FX-Port adapts to BA – interfaces and unifies ▪ access to information ▪ representation of information FX-Port FX-Port unifies configuration for ▪ Access Control and Usage Control of FX-Ports ▪ Composition and Discovery of FX- Ports, FX-Port is a concept to enable interactions of industrial applications in a uniform way The FX Integration Architecture will provide a set of shared services with standardized Interfaces of FX-Ports

# Page 12^page=12
FX Port Services unifies representation of information unifies access to information FX Adapter Services FX Converter Services FX Gate Services adapts to interfaces FX P*,G*,C*,A* : ▪ May be provided by different actors. ▪ Shall be separately deliverable. ▪ Must be combinable. ▪ May not be necessary for every BA-to-BA interaction. unifies configuration and enforcement of rules for ▪ Access Control and Usage Control ▪ Composition of Interactions and Discovery of Interfaces of Port-, Gate-, Converter- and Adapter Services FX-Port generic concept for vertical and horizontal integration 9February 18, 2025

# Page 13^page=13
FX Port 2 FX Con ver ter Se rvices FX Gate Service s FX Adapter Services FX Access & Usa ge Con tro l Se rvice BA / LA 2 (responder) FX Port 1 FX Con ver ter Se rvices FX Gate Service s FX Adapter Services FX Compo sition & Discovery Se rvices FX Access & Usa ge Con tro l Se rvice BA / LA 1 (requester) 1 Gate 1 requests destination of Gate 2 of BA / LA 2 (e.g., via EDC Discovery Service) 2 4 3 5 6 Example operation : BA / LA 1 requests generic operation kind X (e.g., read, write, execute, delete) from BA / LA 2 BA / LA 1 transfers information and requests operation X to Adapter 1. BA / LA 1 interface is used as specified Adapter 1 transfers information of BA / LA 1 to interface of Converter 1 and invokes operation X to Converter 1 Converter 1 converts information of BA / LA 1 to information for exchange and invokes operation X’ to Gate 1 If succeeds Gate 1 transfers information for exchange and invokes operation X’ of Gate 2 Gate 2 enforces access & usage control of operation X’, Converter 2 converts information for exchange to information model of BA / LA 2 and invokes operation X’’ to Adapter 2 BA / LA 2 processes operation X’’ and responds information to Adapter 2. BA / LA 2 interface is used as specified. Adapter 2 transfers information of BA / LA 2 to Converter 2 and invokes operation X’’ to Converter 2 Converter 2 converts information of BA / LA 2 to information for exchange and invokes operation X’ to Gate 2 Gate 2 transfers information for exchange and invokes operation X’ of Gate 1 Gate 1 transfers information for exchange and invokes operation X’ of Converter 1 Converter 1 converts information for exchange to information model of BA / LA 1 and invokes operation X to Adapter 1 Adapter 1 transfers information and confirms operation X to BA / LA 1. Therefor BA / LA 1 interface is used as specified 8 9 110 111 112 113 114 115 Information = operation kind (e.g., read, write, execute, delete) and parameters X, X’, X’’ = same kind but different information model of X 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 7 If succeeds Gate 2 transfers information for exchange via Ports and invokes operation X’ of Converter 2 9 116 110 111 112 114 113 115 116 x y Request Sequence Response Sequence 6 4 The sequence looks complex but this is what is done in one or the other way when integrating applications which have differen t information models and interfaces! FX Compo sition & Discovery Se rvices Adapter 2 transfers information and indicates operation X’’ to BA / LA 2. Therefor BA / LA 2 interface is used as specified FX-Port concept Most generic interaction sequence 10February 18, 2025

# Page 14^page=14
▪ To cover different domain needs ▪ Regulated/non-regulated industries ▪ Automotive/machinery/aerospace// semiconductor/robotics/ etc. ▪ To cover different deployment scenarios ▪ Edge to cloud to edge ▪ Supply-chain to shopfloor and vice versa ▪ To include well established technologies ▪ Communication across data spaces (e.g., EDC, AAS, IDSA) ▪ Communication from data spaces to shop- floor and vice versa (e.g., AAS, OPC UA) ▪ To include brownfield scenarios ▪ Different digitalization concepts are existing (e.g., CESMII Smart Manufacturing Profiles) ▪ Future-oriented & interoperable ▪ Covering regional and domain needs ▪ Open for future concepts (e.g., WoT, 6G) Factory-X – The MX-Port to solve the communication challenges of Manufacturing -X applications! Challenges Concept Implementation 1. Enable business-oriented Factory-X north star qualities (Interoperability, Trust & Security, Scalability, (Data) Sovereignty) 2. Deliver an architecture that enables all Manufacturing-X applications to be to realized 3. Interoperable across scenarios w/ MX-Port architecture & standardized interfaces Individual configurations … …

Standardized Information Standardized Access Type 1: (Passive) Type 2: Re-active (Client / Server) Type 3 : Pro-active (Servient) Standardized Interaction Main characteristics Main added value Leo uses AAS Three AAS types serve different needs

# Page 16^page=16
IEC 63278 Part 1 FX Port AAS specifications and standards therefore to be enhanced by necessary content for AAS Type 3. AAS Type 3 is a promising candidate for Leo - a standardized and I4.0 conformant implementation of the MX-Port concept

# Page 17^page=17
Hercules adds data space features to existing AAS specifications (Type 1 and Type 2) 5 June 2025 14 The Hercules Port is ready for adoption AAS Part 1-5 don’t tell you where the access tokens come from. You and your business partner need that. That’s what DSP is for. DSP doesn’t specify how to authenticate requests flying in from the internet. You and your business partner need a harmonized identity infrastructure. That’s what DCP and its underlying standards are for. # Page 18^page=18
Already PoC existing since HMI 2024 OPC UA is a promising candidate for Orion - a standardized and I4.0 conformant implementation of the MX-Port concept FX Port Services FX Adapter Services FX Converter Services FX Gate Services Available Protocols Available Protocols OPC UA Meta Model Publish / SubscribeClient / Server Built-in Information Models OPC UA Companion Specification Vendor Specific Extentions HTTP(S) NetConf OPC UA TCP UADP Vendor Specific Product Integration Gateway Products Outside of F-X, because already a wide range of available solutions • Synchronous/Asynchronous • Events • Methods • Binary/REST • Objectoriented • Usually on machine type level • Can incooperate AAS SMT Converter: C/L -> P/S UADP DSP/DCP February 18, 2025 15

# Page 19^page=19
Goal Key Results Our focus Lay the technical foundation for interoperability of all projects under the Manufacturing-X umbrella based on requirements of FX Use Cases and general FX consortia qualities. TP4: Interoperable Communication and Information Exchange January 15, 2025 16 ▪ Analysis of general requirements of cross company / cross vertical integration of existing SW applications. ▪ Conceptualization of the necessary transformational steps and deduction of the belonging functional components. ▪ Map the functional components to already existing specifications and standards and derive the FX-Port as the central concept out of it. ▪ Start of specification of the FX Port components Interfaces ▪ Definition of a scalable architecture and their belonging building blocks to integrate SW applications of different vendors and usage scenarios. ▪ Re-use existing specifications and standards (e.g. IEC, Tractus-X, IDTA, OPC- F) to integrate IT and OT cross vertical and cross company. ▪ Integrate existing SW applications unchanged to protect investments and IP of the ecosystem participants. ▪ Support an affordable way for SME to develop their offerings in digitalization. TP-Icon (1,8 x 1,8 cm)

Beyond FX FX-Port generic concept for vertical and horizontal integration 17 Interactions between business applications (“blue” or “red”) will be implemented in a uniform manner using a concept provided by TP4 Uniform access to information (FX Shared Services) Usage View Technical View Functional View Implementation View Requirements FX-Port concept FX-Port components Standardized FX-Port components Standardized FX-Port services TP4 components of integration architecture (level of detail comparable to IEC 63278-2,3,5) IEC 63278 Part 2,3,5 && specs of IDTA Interfaces, SMT && EDC Interfaces W3C standards && specs of IDTA API, SMT && EDC API TP2 requirements for a FX-Port TP4 proposal for FX- Port to be used by TP2 (level of detail comparable to IEC 63278-1) Legacy applications will be integrated in a uniform manner using a concept provided by TP4design by TP4, validation by TP2 Uniform representation of information (FX Connector) Uniform acquisition of information from and distribution of information to legacy applications developed by TP2 developed jointly by TP2 and TP4 FX Shared Services Components FX Converter Components FX IT/OT Integration components Vision beyond FX AAS and EDC Interfaces operations services AAS Submodels Interface operations and IDTA SMT services AAS Asset integration services of legacy AAS Asset related service(s) of legacy FX Port API(s) ▪ FX Composition and Discovery API(s) ▪ FX Access &Usage Ctrl. API(s) FX Gate API(s) FX Converter API(s) FX Adapter API(s) Standardized APIsLegacy APIs

# Page 21^page=21
MX-Port interfaces – Design proposal : Use an asynchronous and event driven interaction scheme for MX-Port components deployable as microservices 18 Supported by ▪ Deployment of MX-Port components as microservices ▪ Standardized and combinable interfaces and APIs of MX-Port components ▪ Standardized interaction scheme between MX-Port components ▪ Message and therefore event driven interaction between MX-Port components ▪ Support of asynchronous interactions Legacy APIs Standardized APIs Standardized MX-Port internal APIs

# Page 23^page=23
MX-Port interfaces – Design proposal : Define MX-Port Messages to support event driven interaction and standardized and flexible combination of MX-Port Interfaces and APIs as well as pre-integration of functions into one service 20 Operations / Notifications ▪ Create / .req,.cfm,.ind,.resp ▪ Delete / .req,.cfm,.ind,.resp ▪ Write / .req,.cfm,.ind,.resp ▪ Read / .req,.cfm,.ind,.resp ▪ Execute / .req,.cfm,.ind,.resp Parameters ▪ Types ▪ Names ▪ Values ▪ … Service Data Unit (SDU) Protocol Data Unit (PDU) MX-Port Messages (MPM) <FPM> ::= <FPMH> <SDU> <PDU-Part> <PDU-Part> ::= <PDU> I MX Port Message Header (MPMH) <MPMH> ::= <EP> <MX Message Type> <EP> ::= <SEP> <REP> <SEP> ::= Endpoint of Source <DEP> ::= Endpoint of Destination <MX Message Type> ::= MXREQ I MXCFM I MXIND I MXRESP <SDU> ::= <MX Operation> <EI> <TI> <MX Operation>::= CREATE I DELETE I WRITE I READ I EXECUTE <EI> ::= <SEID> <REID> <REID>::= <DEID> I <SEID>::= Endpoint ID of Source <DEID>::= Endpoint ID of Destination <TI> ::= Identifier of a Transaction <PDU> ::= <octets> <EOL> <octets> ::= <octet> I <octet> <octets> <octet> ::= <8 Bits> ParametersOperations Notifications MX-Port specific – standardized for interfaces of all MX-Port components Application specific MX-Port Interfaces Interfaces of MX-Port components to support MX-Port Messages
